evidence.scientific_clarity: Dual-axis log-loss + cosine LR clearly shown; monotonic loss drop from ~2.19 to ~0.21 is visible; both axes color-coupled per brief hints.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0 set in script; pdffonts confirms DejaVu Sans as Type 3 (bitmapped), not editable Type 42; default DejaVu font family used throughout.
evidence.palette: Blue (#1f77b4) and red (#d62728) are CVD-safe and well-separated; green/red reserved-for-direction discipline not violated; categorical vs directional use is clean.
evidence.layout_density: 7x4 figsize with tight_layout; spine colors provide visual coupling; legend upper-right does not overlap data; y-range is data-driven with no large empty bands.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure; combined legend placed upper-right; axis spine colors are coupled; caption explicitly flags opposite 'better' direction issue per brief hint.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type 3 (custom/bitmapped) fonts per pdffonts; script missing rcParams pdf.fonttype=42; text is not selectable in PDF.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (18 KB), figure.png (73 KB), figure.svg (45 KB), caption.tex (526 B), and gen_figure.py (1.7 KB) all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names both axes, gives specific numerical range (loss 2.19→0.21, LR starts at 1e-3), notes log scale, mentions axis-color coupling, and explicitly warns about opposite 'better' directions.

overall_strengths: Excellent caption: numerical claims, directional ambiguity warning, and axis-coupling description are all present.
Color and layout are polished: CVD-safe palette, color-coupled spines/labels, tight layout with no wasted space.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0): PDF embeds DejaVu Sans as Type 3 bitmapped glyphs, making text non-selectable and non-editable in vector tools.
Default DejaVu Sans font family retained; a publication-grade figure should specify a neutral serif or sans-serif (e.g., Helvetica/Arial via rcParams) rather than the matplotlib default.
